todays_post.title: Tuesday March 17 | Growth | PAS | Scary | CTA: B
todays_post.content:
# Tuesday March 17 | Growth | PAS | Scary | CTA: B

![Post Image](wed-mar18-image.png)

---

Every hospital says they want a single source of truth.
Almost none achieve it.

Here's what nobody tells you before you start.

**The Problem**

It sounds clean in the boardroom.
"Unified clinical, financial, and operational data."

The CIO nods. The CFO nods. The CMO nods.

Then you go to actually build it.

And you realize: you have 15 hospitals.
3 EMR systems.
Each country has its own compliance requirements: Saudi MOH, JCI standards, UAE DHA reporting.
Legacy ERP that was last upgraded in 2014.
Financial data in one currency format.
Clinical data in two different coding standards.
Operational data that lives in someone's Excel file on a shared drive.

**The Agitation**

Vendors walk in and say "unified data in 90 days."

They haven't tried merging Saudi MOH reporting requirements with JCI accreditation data with a legacy ERP that doesn't have an API.

They haven't dealt with a clinical team in one country that has been using one patient ID format for 10 years and a financial team in another country using a different one.

They haven't had the conversation where you find out that "operational data" in hospital A means bed management, and in hospital B it means pharmacy stock.

90 days becomes 9 months.
9 months becomes a boardroom question about why nothing is working.

**The Solution**

Here's what actually works:

1. Start with the reporting layer, not the data layer. Agree on what the output looks like before you touch the source systems.
2. Assign data ownership per domain. Clinical data has an owner. Financial has an owner. Operational has an owner.
3. Build the compliance map before you build the integration. Each country has different rules on what data can move where.
4. Accept that "unified" doesn't mean "identical." The goal is a common view, not a common database.
5. Pick one use case and make it work completely. Then expand. Not the other way around.

The single source of truth is achievable.
Just not in 90 days.
And not without a leader who's willing to say the hard things early.

What's the most painful data unification challenge you've faced?

..

By the way, I've been documenting my journey managing a $50M hospital transformation across 3 countries. If you're navigating a similar challenge, happy to share what's working. Drop a comment or DM.

#HealthcareIT #DataStrategy #DigitalHealth #HospitalTransformation #PMO

borderline.title: Project Director
borderline.jd_snippet: Key Responsibilities: Oversee all phases of project planning, coordination, and execution to ensure technical, financial, and functional success. Manage budgets, schedules, procurement, and resource allocation to achieve cost efficiency and timely delivery. Lead quality assurance, safety, and risk management initiatives in alignment with company standards. Direct design, engineering, and construction supervision activities, ensuring adherence to specifications. Maintain client relationships, drive value engineering, and manage commercial and contractual obligations. Utilize ERP systems (preferably SAP) for cost control, project accounting, and performance tracking. Provide leadership in team development, dispute resolution, and performance management. Qualifications: Minimum  20 years of experience , including  10+ years in project leadership  within large-scale construction or hospitality projects (GCC experience preferred). Strong background in  engineering, project management, and client relations . Proven track record in delivering complex, high-value developments. Excellent leadership, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.

posts.title: Insurance AI Success: Clarity, Alignment, and Human Judgment
posts.author: Kristinguglielmo
posts.topic: 5 days ago ·The recent Fortune Insurance article, "Most AI investments fail and here's what the winners get right," resonated deeply with my own observations in the insurance sector. It highlights a critical ...
posts.ready_comment:
Here's what I've learned from watching AI projects succeed and fail: they rarely fail because of the tech. They fail because organizations lead with "we need AI" instead of "we need to solve X."

At Saudi German Hospital Group, we ran a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals. When we tied AI investments to real operational pain points and got clinicians actually using the tools, the ROI followed. When we didn't, we just had expensive experiments.

Curious what you're seeing in insurance, where do you think the gap is biggest between what AI promises and what it actually delivers?

posts.author: William Bengtson
posts.topic: 5 days ago ·47% of organizations say non-human identities now outnumber humans. Only 22% can actually see them all. That's the identity crisis hiding inside the AI boom. We, ConductorOne, just released our ...
posts.ready_comment:
What keeps me up at night? Running a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals in Saudi Arabia means managing thousands of connected systems, each with their own identities and access patterns. The challenge isn't just visibility. It's establishing trust at scale without compromising security.

How are you tackling identity governance in your AI projects?

posts.title: AI Drives Clinical Care and Efficiency in HealthTech
posts.author: Gilboamir
posts.topic: 4 days ago ·This week in HealthTech highlights how AI and digital innovation continue to move deeper into clinical care, operations, and patient engagement. From new AI tools supporting clinical decision ...
posts.ready_comment:
What's the real challenge with AI in healthcare? It's not the technology itself - it's making it work across multiple facilities while keeping patients safe.

Running a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals under Saudi Vision 2030 taught me something important: you need to focus on change management just as much as the tech.

How do you balance pushing innovation forward with making sure the organization is ready to handle it?

posts.title: Stryker Hit by Massive Wiper Attack in 79 Countries
posts.author: Ecantech Esolutions
posts.topic: 5 days ago ·200,000 devices. 79 countries. Zero warning. 🛑 Today, the healthcare world woke up to a nightmare. MedTech giant Stryker has been hit by a massive "wiper" attack—and it’s a wake-up call for ...
posts.ready_comment: At this scale, wiper attacks aren't just an IT problem, they're a board-level risk. Running PMO for a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals showed me something: cybersecurity isn't a feature you bolt on, it's a culture you build. The real question is how healthcare leaders balance innovation speed with security fundamentals when threats move faster than procurement cycles ever could.
